entry.title: SAP SOLMAN CHARM Digital Signature Fucntionality
entry.content:
Customer doesn't find any standard solution to enable Digital Signature functionality in ChaRM

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP SOLMAN CHARM Digital Signature Fucntionality
entry.content:
As documented in KBA 1902352 - Digital Signature with Change Request Management (ChaRM) - Solution Manager, there is not an out-of-the-box solution for digital signature within ChaRM. Currently, the only available solutions for this are from consulting.

Digital Signature functionality can be integrated with Focused Build.

entry.title: Does SAP recommend to have same PSE for both SAP SSL Server Standard and SAP SSL Client ?
entry.content: Does SAP recommend to have same PSE for both SAP SSL Server Standard and SAP SSL Client Standard in STRUST ? Can we copy the SSL Server PSE to SSL Client Standard ? If so, is this recommended by SAP ?

entry.title: Re: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
This comment is a placeholder for SAP MBC Multi Bank Connectivity...

3398869 - FAQs about SAP MBC for S/4HANA Public Cloud Customers - SAP for Me

3367596 - FAQ: SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC) - SAP for Me

Product Information | SAP Help Portal

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is an innovative on-demand solution that connects financial institutions and other financial service providers with their corporate customers on a secure network owned and managed by SAP. The network offers multiple services in one single channel while supporting the deployment of new services. As key benefits, the solution simplifies connectivity, automates financial transactions, reduces payment rejection rates, eases reconciliation, and provides enhanced visibility to corporate treasury.

You can connect to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity via SFTP (H2H), EBICS, and SWIFT. Banks can also become member banks and then connect via an API.

Glossary | SAP Help Portal

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity | SAP Help Portal

 

 

 

ERP to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

 

When your ERP is connected to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity, you can review the status of files transferred to banks or FSIs directly in your ERP system. You can even connect several ERP systems if this is your use case. This should be aligned with your business and/or banks and FSIs as part of the onboarding process with your dedicated onboarding team.

You set up the connection between ERP and SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity via the connector for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity. It's a module designed to interact with SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity to standardize the integration. The connector is independent of the technical connectivity from ERP to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity. As part of the onboarding to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity, you need to consider different configurations for the connector for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity. The configurations depend on the ERP system you use. For more information, please see the relevant topics in the Onboarding section.

 

SFTP (H2H)

With this connection, you can send messages from the corporate system to the corporate tenant and then to a bank backend system.

For integrating your tenant directly to your bank, SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity offers connectivity via Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) as it provides simple and effective scalability options.

In this scenario, SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity pushes data to a bank's hosted server, and SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity pulls response data from the bank's server.

In most cases, the bank hosts SFTP servers for message exchange. If this isn't possible, the customer or their service partners might need to host the server. SAP doesn't host SFTP servers.

 

Onboarding Process | SAP Help Portal

 

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity - OnBoarding Resource Center

Using SAP for Me to Provision Systems | SAP Help Portal

Onboarding Process | SAP Help Portal

Connector for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity | SAP Help Portal

Roles and Responsibilities in Onboarding | SAP Help Portal

 

AL11 

In MBC you will need to store Bank Files on the S/4HANA File System and you will need ECS to make a File System available and permissioned and accessible with AL11..

3435333 - AL11 Usage, Restrictions, and Support for HEC/ECS Managed Systems - SAP for Me

3434680 - Configuration for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity - SAP for Me

3544856 - Multi-Bank Connectivity Server Certificate Is Expiring in STRUST - SAP for Me

3525155 - Authorization required for provisioning Multi-Bank Connectivity tenant - SAP for Me - SAP for Me

3367596 - FAQ: SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC) - SAP for Me

3066901 - How to Start Provisioning for a cloud product - SAP for Me - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: I am checking the option of moving / migrating Soluti...
entry.content:
Hi!

I would suggest that you run a SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM to see what you are using in your SolMan system and which scenarios are delivered in the SAP Cloud ALM, see SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM guidance here:
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-sap-cloud-alm/sap-readiness-check-for-sap-cloud-alm.html

See also SAP Activate for Transition to SAP Cloud ALM
https://me.sap.com/roadmapviewer/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapoverviewpage/dbe443042b8f43d085c9e5e221bef757

Then check the content in 

- Transition from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-sap-cloud-alm.html

- Transition to SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-calm-implementation.html

- Transition to SAP Cloud ALM: Guidance for typical customer situations
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-calm-example-scenarios.html

- Selective data transfer from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-sap-cloud-alm/transition-to-calm-implementation.html?anchorId=section_284292647_co

- Guidance for Transitioning from SAP Solution Manager Change Request Management to SAP Cloud ALM Change
& Deployment Management
https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/alm/sap-cloud-alm/Best_Practice_Guide_Transition_ChaRM_to_SAP_Cloud_ALM_v1_08042025.pdf

Hope this helps,

Dolores

entry.title: Re: How to retrieve daily work calendar from EC Timesheet...
entry.content:
Hi Volker,

Reading this scenario with interest...

Is it possible to fill a time container with planned time for future time sheet weeks?  We would like to access the planned time for employees ahead of the current time sheet week. It seems while TV results show the time container results in the UI for future weeks, this data is only transient at that point?

thanks


Phil

entry.title: How to pass BASE64 image data to smartform graphic or any other option to get the image in invoice
entry.content:
I have an image of a QR code which contain data 'A'. I am working on a Smart form, here in the invoice I need to get this data 'A' when the user is scanning the QR code.

The above-mentioned Image is in a BASE64 data. How can I make it appear in invoice through Smart form.

I have tried decoding the data and converting it to Binary, but I was not able to covert that binary to BITMAP data as I got exception saying No BITMAP file. However, even if I get that bitmap data also I couldn't find a way to bind it in graphic node, as there is no option to select graphic type in Smart form.

FM which I have used 

SCMS_BASE64_DECODE_STR

SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY

SAPSCRIPT_CONVERT_BITMAP

entry.title: Re: Issue with L_TO_CREATE_MULTIPLE , Global Data is not coming informed (T333, T340,....)
entry.content:
Hello @luisvazquez 

The T333_LESEN form routine simply reads WM movement definition from T333 and T333T tables:

FORM T333_LESEN USING VALUE(P_LGNUM) VALUE(P_BWLVS).

  SELECT SINGLE * FROM T333
  WHERE LGNUM = P_LGNUM    AND
        BWLVS = P_BWLVS.

  IF SY-SUBRC NE 0.
    MESSAGE E001 WITH P_BWLVS RAISING BWLVS_WRONG.
  ENDIF.

  PERFORM T333T_LESEN USING P_LGNUM P_BWLVS.

ENDFORM.

As you see, the logic is extremely simple. If that doesn't work for you, it means that the WM movement type is not customized for the warehouse number. Check the content of T333.

What is the specific error that you get?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: What Business Catalog is needed to access "maintain s...

entry.title: Analytics Cloud & Business ByDesign Business Content (AP-RC-ANA-SAC) Errors in queries & import jobs
entry.content:
Hi, everyone.

I am currently trying to implement the SAP Business ByDesign business content (AP-RC-ANA-SAC) available in SAP Analytics Cloud. I am using the connector that comes with this business content (SAP_BYD_CONNECTOR), but I am having a problem with some model import jobs. Namely, the premade import jobs for models SAP__BYD_FI_ORDERPROFIT and SAP__BYD_FI_EARNEDPROFIT do not work. They provide the following error: The freehand query is not an entity set or function import" when creating a new Freehand query in SAC

 

I tried remaking the import jobs myself, by copying a query from a model. In the image below, after pressing next with this or a similar configuration, I am met with an infinite loading screen.

 

Thus, I tried making a new query. The problem is that the the tables for the two aforementioned models, RPFINPRFU30_Q0004QueryResults and RPFINPRFU30_Q0006QueryResults, are not visible in the list of tables, no matter what I do.

 

 

Do you have any advice on what I need to do in order to get access to these tables? My knowledge of ByDesign is quite limited, but the connector in SAC seems to be implemented correctly. I also should have access to Corporate Performance Analytics, since this connector does not return an authentication error.


...sapbydesign.com/sap/byd/odata/cpm_corporateperformance_analytics.svc

Can anyone guide How to fix it?

Separately when authenticating i saw this:

/zfe_ui_travel_o4_001116 - this is the abap package; trial account in singapore region.

service key:

{
"uaa": {

entry.title: Re: Fiori Launchpad Plugin (using setHeaderTitle) not working
entry.content: Hi Ankit, were you able to identify the root cause of the issue?? for this error? --> Filed to load JavaScript resource: sap/ushell/plugins/rta/Component-preload.js - sap.ui.ModuleSystem

entry.title: Re: Withholding Tax calculation issue while posting REFX...
entry.content:
Hi Tivahar,

 

Firstly you will need to create 2 withholding tax items in the Term of condition. In the posting item of the term, you will have to choose multiple suppliers, change the layout to bring in withholding tax into the posting terms. Here you will have to individual assign the WHT terms you created to each individual landlord. The layout will not default to ask you the WHT terms when splitting the condition with 2 suppliers.

Regards,

Lakshmi

entry.title: Re: Enhancement before SAVE for Custom object changes
entry.content: Hi Raymond,

entry.title: Re: Enhancement before SAVE for Custom object changes
entry.content: Hi Raymond, I checked the mentioned BAdI, but it is triggering ata the time of TR released.

entry.title: Poland VAT exchange rate in Account Payables process with Purchase order (SAP ECC)
entry.content:
Hi, in Poland if domestic invoice is received in foreign currency, invoice needs to have VAT value in PLN currency (official Polish currency).  Invoice date is not necessarily a match with this VAT f/x rate used in the invoice as it can be based on delivery date.  

Is there a way to solve this in SAP ECC that VAT can have different exchange rate date than invoice date or posting date? 

There is Note for S/4 Hana public cloud version that I think is suitable but I did not find any for ECC.

entry.title: Re: Enhancement before SAVE for Custom object changes
entry.content: Hi Raymond, Thank You for reply. I checked the mentioned BAdI, but it is triggering at the time of TR release. We need before saving the changes to TR.

My client mentioned that  they cannot access the Onboarding Survey which should be available from a Custom Task called "Candidate Experience at Daikin". Here you may see the MDF Object name (Recruiting survey link (cust_surveyRecruiting).

We cannot reach that even trying to access the link to Survey from our internal sharepoint (it's throwing an error this form does not exist. Please make sure the link is correct. 

Regards

entry.title: Re: Sourcing and Procuremant
entry.content:
Hi,

It is not available.

For managing discounts or differences, SAP S/4HANA might include features like Discount Management via the SAP Business Network, which offers functionality Sourcing and Procurement - Discount Management (42K) to transfer payment proposals and manage cash discount terms. Such features help in providing insights into payment and discount dynamics but may not directly report differences between quotation and order amounts for specific items.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Become an Early Adopter for Joule in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
If I understood it correctly, in a nutshell, is that this early access program is for customers only.

Partners, who are often asked by customers and expect us to inform them about new technology, must wait, or convince a customer of ours to sign up.

entry.title: Re: how to make an Optional inspection characteristic Required after a certain number of inspection
entry.content: @memem : Yes thanks. That is the approach which finally worked for me. I had been struggling with it because I had been maintaining the char as optional but once I mentioned it as required and then applied DMR at char level, it worked perfectly.

entry.title: Re: BOP - S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, materials with project stock might not behave the same way as regular stock during the Backorder Processing (BOP) job due to the inherent restrictions and configurations. This behavior you observed is indeed related to how SAP handles project stocks in comparison to regular stocks.

How Project Stock Works

Project stock is treated distinctly from plant stock, and its availability is tightly linked to enterprise projects. The primary goal of project stock is to ensure that materials are consumed only by the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) elements Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2108 and assigned orders to which the project stock belongs, enhancing planning efficiency and project procurement effectiveness source 1.

Availability Check Configuration

The difference in behavior between project stock and regular stock during BOP can be influenced by availability check configurations, scope of check, and other setting variants used in your system.

Things to Consider

  • Scope of Check: Ensure that the scope of check is properly configured to include project stock. Sometimes project stock might require specific settings to be included in the availability check.
  • Configuration Settings: Check if there are settings in the Availability Check or Backorder Processing that might apply only to regular stock and not project stock.

Possible Adjustments

You may consider reviewing and adjusting the configuration settings such as:

  • Order Promising Strategies: Supply Chain - Backorder Processing (BOP) Evaluate if the settings for project stock in ‘Order Promising’ and availability checks are correctly set source 12.
  • Backorder Management: Ensure that high-running materials settings are well configured Supply Chain - High-Running Materials in Backorder Processing for project stock and adjust the BOP configurations appropriately source 13.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Enhancing IDoc Filtering in SAP BD64
entry.content: Informative! Just a question - is it possible for dynamic idoc filtering? For example if you put filter 'W101' for plant it will only send the idoc with that plant. But how about all plants that start with W, like 'W*', would that be possible?

entry.title: Re: Name of iOS app is not showing correctly on iOS and MacOS
entry.content: Excellent to hear, thanks for confirming @ChantalChi

entry.title: Re: Can't instantiate a fragment after Destory it
entry.content:
I suggest to avoid destroying it to avoid that error.

openDocumentaleDialog: function () {
    if (!this.oDocumentaleDialog) {
        Fragment.load({
            name: "webapp.fragment.DocumentaleDialog",
            id: this.createId("dialogDocumentale"),
            controller: this
        }).then((oFragment) => {
            this.oDocumentaleDialog = oFragment;
            this.getView().addDependent(this.oDocumentaleDialog);
            this.oDocumentaleDialog.open();
        });
    } else {
        this.oDocumentaleDialog.open();
    }
}

 Your assumption "To ensure good performance, I simply destroy it" — is partially correct as recreating the fragment from scratch does have a cost. And to be fair, a popup is usually something very minor when it comes to performance and memory overhead.

entry.title: Fetching the Count from OData service response
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I am building an application using SAP Build Apps, integrating it with SAP SuccessFactors Platform with OData services.

On a view, I turned on the Include total count feature from the Paging option, found on the Get Record Collection flow logic.

With this, I get the data from SAP SuccessFactor, in below format:
{
"d": {
"results": [ ... ],
"__count": "381"
}
}

Can anyone please help me understand, how can I use/bind the value coming in the field - __count , so that I can display the count of the records?

Regards,

Chandan

entry.title: Re: Consuming data from SAP Datasphere into Power BI via ODBC connector
entry.content:
Hi  @vignesh3027 ,
Can you help me with importing Variables(Standard variable to be precise) created in Datasphere ? 

I tried the following steps :

 https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/passing-values-in-input-parameters-variables-of-datasphere-model-from-power/ba-p/13988127 .


But, I keep running into the following error = > DataSource.Error: ODBC: ERROR [S1000] [SAP AG][LIBODBCHDB DLL][HDBODBC] General error;303 invalid DATE, TIME or TIMESTAMP value: SQL Error 

Details:
    DataSourceKind=Odbc
    DataSourcePath=dsn

More on this, I have tried disabling and enabling global/current page Time intelligence for auto detection of Data field.

entry.title: Re: Template Allocation and Splitting Structure
entry.content: Hi, I am not familiar with the formula you are using. I would suggest looking at the following function: K_ABC_GET_FILL_ACDOCA_VALS. There should be some sap notes that suggests some uses. In regards to the splitting, I can assure you that there should be no difference between using a template or other posted direct activity type. May I suggest to verify if all accounts are distributed equally or only one or a few? Usually it splits the accounts that are not mapped in the splitting schema (or cost element group if used)

entry.title: Re: Modeling an advanced Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: @abarros123 if you mean selecting nodes/leafs from a hierarchy in a variable: not at this point. What is on the roadmap in this direction (but not the same) is hierarchy node selection for restricted measures

entry.title: Re: Poland VAT exchange rate in Account Payables process...
entry.content:
Is the information in KBA 1907011 - Poland Exchange Rate Requirements complementary information compliant?

  • 1330330 - Poland Exchange Rate Requirements

entry.title: Re: New Webinar Series: Architecting Your SAP Business Data Cloud Journey – From Data to Intelligenc
entry.content: Hi @EsaNieminen  - the registered participants will get access to the recordings and the slides via email. We will be posting synopsis of the session as blog posts in the coming days. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: CDS view performance Annotation for CDS view performance
entry.content:
The blog post brings up some interesting annotations, which I totally ignored in the last years.

There is no magic turbo switch!
please refer to the comment of topic expert @AndreaUS

If you experience varying performance I would see machine resources as the first reason.
If you experience totally bad performance I would use a trace and planviz  to find the root cause. Spoiler alert: 99 % chance your custom view was built in the wrong way. PEBKAC 😅 

kind regards,

Christian

entry.title: Re: EDI - Sales Orders
entry.content: To add to this answer you can configure it so failed IDocs trigger workflow for appropriate business users too in order to correct data issues, which is what we do here.

entry.title: ABAP CDS: Intent based navigation isn't working properly in draft-enabled Apps
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am currently facing an issue with intent-based navigation in ABAP CDS and Fiori Elements.

I’m using the navigation in a composition of the header table. So, when I open a detail entry of the header table and navigate further using intent-based navigation, everything works perfectly — as long as I’m not in edit mode. As soon as I switch to edit mode, the property "IsActiveEntity=false" is also passed along in the intent-based navigation. This can be correct if a draft entry exists for the navigation target. However, if there is no draft entry in the navigation target, the navigation leads to a dead end:

Here's the code of the parent-entity (Please note that the underlying service is an OData V4 service):

@EndUserText.label: 'Parent entity foo'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

define root view entity ZP_PARENT_FOO
    provider contract transactional_query
    as projection on ZI_PARENT_FOO

{   
    
  key uuid                                          as UUID,
      somefield                                     as Some_Field,
      validfrom                                     as Valid_From,
      validto                                       as valid_To,

      _ChildFoo : redirected to composition child ZP_CHILD_FOO
      
}

Here's the code of the child-entity:

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Child entity Foo'

define view entity ZP_CHILD_FOO
    as projection on ZI_CHILD_FOO
{

    @UI.hidden: true
    key uuid                as UUID,
    
    @UI: {
          lineItem:       [ { position: 10,
                              type: #WITH_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION, 
                              semanticObject: 'SomeOtherApp',
                              semanticObjectAction: 'manage',
                              semanticObjectBinding: [{  localElement: 'FooUUID', element: 'UUID'} ] ,
                              requiresContext: false 
                          } ]
        }
    key Foo_uuid       as FooUUID,
    
    some_field         as SomeField,
        
    @UI.hidden: true
    last_changed_at         as LastChangedAt,
    
    _ParentFoo : redirected to parent ZP_PARENT_FOO
    
}

Is there a specific annotation that can be used to override the “IsActiveEntity” property?

Many thanks in advance and best regards
Leif

@Andre_Fischer 

SAP BTP ABAP environment ABAP Development

entry.title: Re: SF - Story Reporting - Time Tracking - Display of weekdays

Hi Pedro

Unforunately I haven't found a solution nor workaround. I am still looking for a way to "show" the weekdays. 

Kind regards, 

Alexandra

entry.title: Dismantle Machine
entry.content:
Q. what is the standard SAP Process of Dismantle Machine 

How to Dismantle Machine With Serial Number Profile as per Confirmation Component,

entry.title: Re: PO change in SAP
entry.content: SPED is not PO

entry.title: How to update fields in the Additional Information section using import template in Manage Workforce
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support, 

 

We need to update certain fields in the Additional information section of Personal information in the Manage Workforce app. However, using the Worker template, we are not able to update fields like Middle Name and Initials in the Additional Information section. How can we update the Additional information section fields using import template. 

 

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to update fields in the Additional Information se...
entry.content:
Hi, 

Thank you for reaching out and for your patience as we address your concern.

At this time, it is not possible to update the Additional Information section using the import template within the Manage Workforce application.

If you only need to update a small number of users, you can do so manually using the Edit button in Manage Workforce, provided the Entry Source is 'W'.

For a larger number of users, we recommend using the SOAP API. You can find more information here:

PersonalInformation | SAP Help Portal

Please also note that with the next release, JSON support will be available in Manage Workforce, offering more features. 

Thank you.

entry.title: The client is locked for data import by client copy
entry.content:
I have dev box which has 2 client. 100 and 200. 100 is golden client and 200 is testing. I want to move the TR's using SCC1 in 200 after creating the TR in 100. This is only possible when you have your 200 client open. 

How can we move the TR in SCC1 in client 200 but with client closed?

entry.title: Re: SFAPI Data Dictionary
entry.content: Thanks a lot Xavier, your inputs are really helpful.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Readiness Check for an enhancement running in...
entry.content:
@EnnoWulff 

Hi Enno,

thanks for your response! In the meantime we were able to update table TRNSPACE and switch the role from "P" to "C" for the problematic entry /0SJ1P/. I'm now at least able to get ATC-results when I run a check from within SE84 for the enhancement.

What still doesn't work is to set up a check run based on an object set which includes the enhancements I'd like to run the checks for. I'm including both the enhancement names in our Z-namespace as well as the enhanced SAP objects SAPMV45A and SAPMV50A but compared to a NW 750 system I can no longer include enhanced objects:

Regardless of which combination of object set or free object selection I try, the check ends after a few seconds as if it's not even looking for any objects to check.

I'll take another stab at this next week but will most likely raise an OSS-incident then.

entry.title: Path to Success: Discovering Hidden Gems in Performance and Goals
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the option "Register Now" button or "Yes" to "Will you be attending?" will NOT register you for the event. To register for the event, please REGISTER HERE in order to receive the event invite and login details. 

 

Session Information:

Explore the untapped potential of our performance and goals experiences in this enlightening webinar. Learn about the newest features and leading-edge practices that can propel your organization forward. Get ready to discover capabilities you never knew existed!
 
AMER/EMEA Session
Wednesday, 17th September 2025
10:00 EDT USA | Puerto Rico
16:00 CEST Europe

REGISTER HERE!

 

If you have any questions, please get in touch with the Program Manager: 

Vanesa Medricka - vanesa.medricka@sap.com
Sharon O'Neil - sharon.o-neill@sap.com

Appreciate any feedback if you have tried it 🙂

entry.title: Configuring SAP Start Step by Step
entry.content:
Dear All

We want to access the SAP Public Cloud System through SAP Mobile Start and having SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition , after manual configuration , required Apps are still not showing. Please suggest , what is missing or any step by step guide. 

Following activities have been performed.

  1. SAP Public Cloud system is registered within System Landscape of BTP Global Account.
  2. Within SAP Public Cloud scenario - SAP_COM_0647 is also configured to outbound (https://api.cf.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com:443/)
  3. Formation having SAP Build Work Zone and Public Cloud System
  4. SAP Build Work Zone Site is available , however no content is showing.

entry.title: Re: Dismantle Machine
entry.content: Start with KBA 3527138 - Dismantling and Moving Equipment in EAM

entry.title: Re: US Form I-9 Concepts for SuccessFactors Onboarding 2.0
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have a huge issue related to I-9 wherein US users are requesting that the field Employer's Business or Organization Name and Employer's Business or Organization Address, City or Town, State, ZIP Code fields should have the details about the HR who is responsible for completing Section2 form. 

For example,  lets say that the candidate is hired for a legal entity ABC and the HR who is responsible for completing the section2 of I-9 form is in XYZ legal entity then in the form where the fields Employer's Business or Organization Name and Employer's Business or Organization Address, City or Town, State, ZIP Code fields should have the details about the XYZ legal entity. How can we achieve this? Have any of you faced similar issues and how did you overcome ?

Let me know. SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

entry.title: How to set-up file paths while using ABAP Dataflow in SAP DS
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We are using SAP DS for ETL and Data migration activities. We are using ABAP Data flow in-order to extract the data from SAP Applicaiton server. where we are having an issue to locate the .dat files which gets generated and stored in the path defined under Datastore settings.

We would like to know the best practices and infra setup regarding ABAP Dataflows so that we will not have any issue in Quality and Prod migration run. Any help would be appreciated a lot!

Thank you in advance!

SAP Data Services Basis Technology

entry.title: Re: Can we customise SAC standard menu options available in home screen?

entry.title: Re: Business Partner Issue- CRM004 vendor master
entry.content: Hi all, I hope you're doing well. While trying to maintain the identification data for CRM004 (R/3 Supplier Number) in the Business Partner, I received an error, and the system did not allow me to make any changes. To enable this, I would like to adjust the customizing under the following path: SPRO → SAP Implementation Guide → Cross-Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner → Basic Settings → Identification Numbers → Define Identification Categories, so that the entry is no longer display-only. Before doing so, I wanted to ask if there is a specific reason why SAP has defined this entry as display-only by default? Would changing this setting lead to any functional or integration issues later on? Thank you in advance for your support and clarification. Best regards, Razieh

Jaya

Business partner with GUID XYZ does not exist

Do you have any idea what I might have missed?

Thank you.

entry.title: How to make visible customer data item level tab for PO with item category E
entry.content:
Hello,
in our purchase orders we have a customer data tab at item level which was created with enhancement MM06E005. This is working  fine except when we use item category E (Enhanced Limit), where the tab disappears.

I have checked BADI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST and method FIELDSELECTION_ITEM. I do not find any existing coding to make this tab visible/invisible. I tried this code. but when it comes to item category E program does not stop at breakpoint in BADI. Also I try breakpoint in enhancement exits and does not stop either.

    l_changeable = im_header->is_changeable( ).
    LOOP AT ch_fieldselection REFERENCE INTO DATA(ld_fieldselection)
                                  WHERE metafield = mmmfd_cust_10.
      IF l_changeable IS INITIAL.
        ld_fieldselection->fieldstatus = '*'. " Display
      ELSE.
        ld_fieldselection->fieldstatus = '+'. " Change
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

In transaction SPRO there are no options to handle customer data tab visibility.

Anyone has faced this issue?

Thanks.

entry.content:
Hi Team,

How to default Maintenance Plant in component section of Work Order?

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Re: Speed Up Check-Ins: AI-Assisted Goods Receipt Analysis in SAP S/4HANA TM 2023
entry.content: @Saurabh : as mentioned in pre-requisite, you need an S/4HANA TM 2023 system on private cloud. There is an interactive value journey  which may be interesting as well for you.

entry.title: Plan Activity rates coming as Actuals in KSII
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have maintained a splitting structure for each activity type, and in transaction KSS2, the actual costs are being accurately distributed to each activity type. However, when executing KSII, the indirect activity type (used in Template Allocation) is not generating an actual cost rate; instead, the system is applying the plan rate from KP26. This occurs despite the fact that the actual quantity is correctly captured and the cost splitting has been performed successfully. In contrast, for all direct activity types (used in the Master Recipe), the actual cost rate is calculated correctly as Actual Cost divided by Actual Quantity.

Kindly help us resolving this issue

Regards

Merin

entry.title: External work centers to our operations in the work order
entry.content:
Hi Team,

How can we assign external work centers to our operations in the work order

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Is there a way to reprice PO items in mass?
entry.content:
We've looked into the options but on Public Cloud there’s unfortunately no possibility to reprice POs in bulk it seems. 
The only available option is to reprice them per item line, and that’s only possible if there are no follow-up documents linked yet.

Is this correct?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to reprice PO items in mass?

entry.title: Fiori: Extensions and Custom Actions with V2 OData - CAPM
entry.content:
This blog explains how to create a List Report page with a custom action that opens a fragment. The fragment displays data fetched via an OData read operation. Prerequisites include knowledge of Fiori, CAPM, Extensions, and OData.

After creating a List report page add a Custom Action from Actions where you need to create an Action.

Add a fragment in extension folder, extension folder will be created in the previous step (Adding Custom Action)

ProductList.fragment.xml:

<core:FragmentDefinition
	xmlns="sap.m"
	xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
	xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
	xmlns:layout="sap.ui.layout">

	<Dialog
		id="ShowTablePop"
		title="Products"
		contentWidth="80%"
		contentHeight="225px"
		draggable="true"
		resizable="true"
		core:require="{handler: 'purchasereport/ext/controller/Handlers'}">

		<layout:VerticalLayout>
			<Table
				id="productResponsiveTable"
				items="{productDataModel>/ProductItems}"
				inset="false"
				width="100%">

				<columns>
					<Column>
						<header>
							<Label text="Product ID" />
						</header>
					</Column>
					<Column>
						<header>
							<Label text="Product Code" />
						</header>
					</Column>
					<Column>
						<header>
							<Label text="Product Name" />
						</header>
					</Column>
					<Column>
						<header>
							<Label text="Product Price" />
						</header>
					</Column>
					<Column>
						<header>
							<Label text="Product Manufacturer" />
						</header>
					</Column>
				</columns>

				<items>
					<ColumnListItem type="Active">
						<cells>
							<Text text="{productDataModel>Product_ID}" />
							<Text text="{productDataModel>Product_Code}" />
							<Text text="{productDataModel>Product_name}" />
							<Text text="{productDataModel>Product_price}" />
							<Text text="{productDataModel>Product_manufacturer}" />
						</cells>
					</ColumnListItem>
				</items>
			</Table>
		</layout:VerticalLayout>

		<endButton>
			<Button text="Close" press="handler.onCloseTableDialog" />
		</endButton>
	</Dialog>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

 

CAP projects expose OData V4 by default, but the V2 adapter can be enabled for compatibility and simplified integration. Install the V2 adapter via @cap-js-community/odata-v2-adapter for CAP OData V2 support.

For more info, refer:

  1.      https://www.npmjs.com/package/@cap-js-community/odata-v2-adapter

Now, configure the additional data source in `manifest` to reference the V2-adapted service.

manifest:

 "dataSources": {
      "mainService": {
        "uri": "/odata/v4/backend-mstr/",
        "type": "OData",
        "settings": {
          "annotations": [],
          "odataVersion": "4.0"
        }
      },
      "v2EnabledModel": {
        "uri": "/odata/v2/backend-mstr/",
        "type": "OData",
        "settings": {
          "annotations": [],
          "odataVersion": "2.0"
        }
      }
    },
  "": {
        "dataSource": "mainService",
        "preload": true,
        "settings": {
          "operationMode": "Server",
          "autoExpandSelect": true,
          "earlyRequests": true
        }
      },
      "v2EnabledModel": {
        "dataSource": "v2EnabledModel",
        "preload": true,
        "settings": {
          "operationMode": "Server",
          "autoExpandSelect": true,
          "earlyRequests": true
        }
      },

 Once the Action is triggered, the fragment will be opened and retrieve the OData model in your controller extension using a function to fetch data and bind it to a JSON Model.

The controller fetches the `v2EnabledModel` and performs a read on `/Products` and set to the JSON model

Handlers.js:

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/m/MessageToast",
      "sap/ui/core/Fragment",
    "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel"
], function(MessageToast, Fragment, JSONModel) {
    'use strict';

    return {
        DialogRef: null,
        OpenFragment: function(oEvent) {
            var oController = this;
            if (!this.DialogRef) {
                Fragment.load({
                    id: "ViewData",
                    name: "purchasereport.ext.fragment.ProductList",
                    controller: oController
                }).then(function (oFragment) {
                    oController.DialogRef = oFragment;
                    _readProductsEntity(oController);
                    oController.DialogRef.open();
                }.bind(this));
            } else {
                oController.DialogRef.open();
            }
        },
        onCloseTableDialog: function () {
            if (this.DialogRef) {
                this.DialogRef.close();
            }
        }
    };
    function _readProductsEntity(oController) {

        var productDataModel = new JSONModel()
        oController.DialogRef.setModel(productDataModel, "productDataModel")
        var productDataModel = oController.DialogRef.getModel("productDataModel");
        var oModel = oController.getModel("v2EnabledModel");
        oModel.read("/Products", {
            success: function (oData) {
                productDataModel.setData({ ProductItems: oData.results });
            },
            error: function () {
                MessageToast.show("Error reading cancellation data");
            }
        });
    }
});

 

Output :

entry.title: Reusable Logger Class in ABAP – Simple Logging in Reports
entry.content:
Introduction: 

In many ABAP reports or background jobs, we often write WRITE: or MESSAGE statements just for debugging or tracking execution. 

  • But what if we had a reusable logger class that: 
    • Keeps all logs organized 
    • Categorizes logs (INFO, ERROR, SUCCESS, etc.) 
    •  Works in any report or enhancement 
    • Allows us to download or display logs 

This post shows how to use a global logger class to make that happen — clean, simple, and reusable across your SAP system. 

  • What We’re Building 
    We’ll create and use a global class ZGCL_LOGGER with these methods: 
    • ADD_LOGS( ) – Add a new log entry with type and message 
    • DOWNLOAD_LOGS( ) – Download the collected logs to a text file 
    • DISPLAY_LOGS( ) - Display the collected logs in a ALV
    • CLEAR_LOGS( ) - Clear the collected logs
  • We can use this logger in any report, like this: 

             DATA(obj_log) = NEW zgcl_logger( ). "Create a object for Log class and access the methods of it"
             obj_log->add_logs( i_type = 'INFO' i_message = 'Program started' ). 

Demo Program: ZREP_TEST_LOGS 
Below is a simple report that fetches flight records (SFLIGHT) and logs the steps.

REPORT ZREP_TEST_LOGS.

DATA: lt_flights TYPE TABLE OF sflight.

START-OF-SELECTION.

 data(obj_log) = new zgcl_logger( ).

  obj_log->add_logs( i_type = 'INFO' i_message = 'Program started' ).

  SELECT * FROM sflight INTO TABLE lt_flights UP TO 10 ROWS.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    obj_log->add_logs(
                      EXPORTING
                      i_type = 'ERROR'
                      i_message = 'No flight records found' ). " adding the logs into Log Table
  ELSE.
    obj_log->add_logs(
                     EXPORTING
                     i_type = 'SUCCESS'
                     i_message = |Fetched { lines( lt_flights ) } flight records| ). " adding the logs into Log Table
  ENDIF.

  obj_log->add_logs( EXPORTING
                     i_type = 'INFO' 
                     i_message = 'Processing complete' ).

  obj_log->display_logs( ). " displaying the logs in ALV

*  obj_log->download_logs( ). " downloading the logs into local system

   obj_log->clear_logs( ). " clear the logs / optional

Sample Log Output: 

 

  • About the Logger Class – ZGCL_LOGGER 
    Here's a simplified overview of what the class contains: 

 Private Attributes

  • LT_LOG – Internal table to store logs (timestamp, type, message, user)
  • LS_LOG – Work area used to build individual log entries

Public Instance Methods                                     

 

  • ADD_LOGS( ) – Appends a new log entry to the internal log table

     

  • DOWNLOAD_LOGS( ) – Uses CL_GUI_FRONTEND_SERVICES=>GUI_DOWNLOAD to download logs as a .txt file 

     

  • DISPLAY_LOGS( ) – Displays logs in ALV using REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY

     

  • CLEAR_LOGS( ) – Clears the log table after display/download

     

  • Why we declare LT_LOG and LS_LOG as private to:
    •  Internal data is protected from outside modification.
    • Only methods of the class (like ADD_LOGS, DOWNLOAD_LOGS) can modify or read the logs.
    • Prevents accidental or uncontrolled changes to the log data from outside the class.
  • This keeps your class robust and safe.
  • Reuse It Anywhere 
    Once your class is in SE24 (ZGCL_LOGGER), we can reuse it in: 
    • Reports (SE38) 
    • Enhancements (BADI/User-Exit) 
    •  BAPIs or RFCs 
    •  Background Jobs 
    • Unit Tests 

  No need to rewrite log logic again! 

  • Final Thoughts 
    A simple global logger like this improves: 
    • Code readability 
    • Debugging experience 
    • Standardization across your team

 Why we Use Logs Instead of  MESSAGES?

  • Messages go away after execution — logs can be saved or reviewed later.
  • Messages stop execution (sometimes) — logs don’t interrupt the user.
  •  Logs help in debugging batch jobs or background processes (where we can't see messages directly).

  Final Summary:

  • The ZGCL_LOGGER/any Logger class is a reusable ABAP utility that helps developers log messages (info, errors, success, etc.) during program execution in a clean, structured way.
    It simplifies debugging, improves traceability, and replaces scattered WRITE: or MESSAGE statements with organized logging that can be displayed or downloaded.
  • Perfect for reports, enhancements, and background jobs where we need clear execution tracking.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Custom Fields and Logic app error
entry.content: are you sure you logon to the correct client from fiori launchpad?

entry.title: Re: Fiori Custom Fields and Logic app error
entry.content: Yes, I am.

entry.title: Re: Annotation @AbapCatalog.extensibility.allowNewDatasou...
entry.content:
your understanding is just wrong.

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/extensibility-enablement-for-cds-views

entry.title: Re: Configuring SAP Start Step by Step
entry.content:
Hello @adnanmaqbool 

Are there any required roles or groups that must be explicitly assigned for apps to appear in Mobile Start or Work Zone?

Yes. The following must be in place:

  • Business Roles in S/4HANA Public Cloud must be assigned to the end user that include the relevant business catalogs (e.g., SAP_FIN_BC_AP_PROC_MC).
  • In SAP Build Work Zone, assign the user to a workspace or site that includes federated content or manually created content.
  • Ensure users have the SAP_BR_USER role (or equivalent) and necessary IAM roles in BTP (e.g., Workzone_Standard_User) to view content.
  • For Mobile Start, ensure that users are assigned the MobileUser role in the SAP Cloud Identity Services trust setup (or in the BTP subaccount).

Please refer to the below link

SAP Mobile Start Integration with SAP Build Work Zone and S/4HANA Cloud 

SAP Note 3081996 – SAP Mobile Start: Known issues and troubleshooting

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Gigya - How to capture phone number in OOTB phoneNumb...
entry.content:
Hi @S_Sarkar 

Users can register with their phone numbers on the Mobile Login screen, their phone numbers will be saved to the phoneNumber field in the Accounts Object.

Since a verification process is required for inputting a phone number, you cannot map the phoneNumber field to an element in the UI Builder, but only use the preconfigured input element in the Mobile Login screen.

If you really need to capture the phone numbers on the registration screen, you might want to use the OOTB profile.phones.number field.

For more details on the phoneNumber field, please refer to Phone Number Login

Hope that helps 🙂

entry.title: Re: ABAP BTP Cloud, Download files from ABAP program to local PC
entry.content:
@jmvenezia  can you check the contents of DB table zfio_files and validate if all fields are populated correctly.

What system are you using  (BTP/On premise)

Also how did you install the code, 

Also try with OData 4 service

entry.title: Query Regarding Cost Visibility in Actual Cost Report – SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi

We are currently operating on SAP Public Cloud, where the production order is triggered from the revenue project, utilizing Q type stock.

We have processed GRNs for certain components against the purchase order linked to the production order within this project. In this context, I would appreciate clarification on whether these costs should be visible in the actual cost report for the project.

Additionally, could you confirm the GL against which these costs would be reflected—whether it aligns with the GL planned for cost allocation or the GL designated for budget planning?

Furthermore, I would like to understand the report’s contents upon the closure, confirmation, and settlement of the production order.

Looking forward to your insights.

entry.title: Re: Reusable Logger Class in ABAP – Simple Logging in Reports
entry.content: For application log, we could also read Standard or Custom Class for Application Logs? – Part 1

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Document Management and Time Management - 2 events
entry.content:
Hi Susanne, I can see you are registered and should have received a link now.

There is a manual approval process behind the event, hence it was not instant.

If you have not received the URL yet, let me know.

Thanks,

entry.title: How to maintain the SOBES field for specific combinations of SOBSL and Plant in Customizing?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have a requirement where I need to maintain the SOBES (Special Procurement Type) field in Customizing for specific combinations of SOBSL and Plant.

I’m not entirely sure where exactly this can be maintained or if there’s a dependency table or configuration path that controls this logic.

Could someone point me in the right direction? Ideally, I’m looking for:

The correct IMG path or table where this setting can be maintained

Thank

entry.title: Missing SAP Service and Asset Manager files to download in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Hi! 

I need to download the SAP Service and Asset Manager files and MDK to debug some issues. But i couldn't find the documments in Download center. 

I follow this steps, but couldn't find.

Search By Category → SAP Mobile Solutions → SAP SERVICE AND ASSET MANAGER → MDK PLUG-IN SDK.

 

Best Regards, 

Rafael Eij Umeda

SAP Service and Asset Manager

entry.title: Help Global System Settings FIN-CS
entry.content:
Hello Experts!

The options:

  • Reclassification: Selection Object in Trigger

  • Validation in SAP S/4HANA

  • Breakdown Category: Selection Object in Maximum Selection

  • Currency Translation: Selection Object

  • Use Item Role Attribute

do not appear in the Global System Settings (Transaction CXB3), and SAP is asking me to change them before upgrading to S/4HANA 2023.

However, I don't understand how to do this, because in Note 3326805, it says to activate these fields, but they do not appear in this transaction (CXB3) . I would like to understand the relevance of this, whether I really need to change it, and how to do so.

Thank you very much!

Attached is how CXB3 appears for me.
